serving.natural_language_portion: One standard ebi croquette piece weighs approximately 60 g and is typically served as a snack, appetiser, or side dish in Japanese cuisine.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Breaded fried shrimp (FDC ID 175180) and Potato croquette, breaded, fried (FDC ID 172052), SR Legacy composite estimate
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact ebi croquette entry exists in USDA FoodData Central, Singapore HPB Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries: breaded fried shrimp (FDC ID 175180) and breaded fried potato croquette (FDC ID 172052), blended in approximate proportions (60% shrimp filling, 40% panko-potato shell) consistent with a Japanese-style ebi croquette. Input record values (provenance: ai_generated, source: grabfood) were cross-checked against this USDA composite and retained where consistent. Sodium value of 305.6 mg/100g is plausible for a seasoned, breaded fried product. All values treated as estimated. The original input grabfood source is not an approved primary nutrient source and has not been used as evidence; USDA FoodData Central is the sole nutrient authority for this record.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021: a systematic review. American Journal of Clinical Nutrition. 2021;114(5):1625-1632.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for ebi croquette exists in the University of Sydney GI Database or the Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the closest analogous food category: panko-breaded deep-fried foods with a mixed starch-protein filling. Panko breadcrumbs and wheat flour coating are the dominant carbohydrate contributors; their GI is analogous to white bread derivatives (GI ~70) moderated by the fat and protein content of the shrimp filling, yielding an estimated composite GI of approximately 65. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 11.64) / 100 ≈ 7.6, where carbohydrate per 60 g serving = 19.4 × 0.60 = 11.64 g. This is an analogous estimate; a direct GI measurement for ebi croquette has not been published in any approved source as of 2026-04-17.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 305.6 mg/100g and saturated fat 5.6 g/100g from breaded deep-fried preparation
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit ebi croquette to one piece occasionally, avoid adding soy sauce or mayonnaise dips, and choose lower-sodium accompaniments to keep total meal sodium within the 2,000 mg/day target.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined carbohydrate from panko breadcrumbs and wheat flour (19.4 g/100g, estimated GI ~65) with low dietary fibre (1.7 g/100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should restrict portion to one piece (60 g, approximately 11.6 g carbohydrate, GL ~7.6), pair with non-starchy vegetables, and avoid sweet dipping sauces to limit post-meal glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 5.6 g/100g and trans fat 0.3 g/100g from deep-frying, combined with cholesterol 50 mg/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should treat ebi croquette as an occasional food, limit to one piece per sitting, and prioritise grilled or steamed seafood preparations to reduce saturated and trans fat intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ebi Croquette (エビコロッケ, Ebi Korokke) is a Japanese-style deep-fried snack consisting of a seasoned shrimp (ebi) filling — often combined with a creamy béchamel or mashed potato base — encased in a panko breadcrumb coating and deep-fried in vegetable oil until golden brown and crispy. The exterior is crunchy and light due to the coarse panko crumbs, while the interior is soft, moist, and savoury with a distinct sweet-briny shrimp flavour. Ebi croquettes are a popular yoshoku (Western-influenced Japanese) dish served as a snack, bento component, appetiser, or side dish across Japan and Japanese restaurants in Southeast Asia, including Singapore. A single piece weighs approximately 60 g and provides around 160 kcal, 5 g protein, 10.7 g fat, and 11.6 g carbohydrate. The deep-fried preparation results in moderate saturated fat (5.6 g/100g) and sodium (305.6 mg/100g), making it a moderate-concern food for individuals managing hypertension, type 2 diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The panko coating contributes refined carbohydrates with an estimated medium glycemic index (~65), and the low dietary fibre content (1.7 g/100g) means blood glucose response should be managed through portion control. Shrimp provides some lean protein and trace omega-3 fatty acids, partially offsetting the pro-inflammatory effects of deep-frying.
common_user_queries: How many calories in an ebi croquette?
Is ebi croquette high in sodium?
Is ebi croquette suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: No exact ebi croquette entry exists in any approved primary source (USDA FoodData Central, Singapore HPB Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy composite entries for breaded fried shrimp (FDC ID 175180) and potato croquette (FDC ID 172052), cross-checked against the input record (ai_generated, grabfood provenance, not used as a primary source). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient breakdown, and moisture data for a Japanese-style ebi croquette are not available as a discrete entry in USDA FoodData Central, Singapore HPB Foodo, or other approved sources as of 2026-04-17. The input record (grabfood, ai_generated) did not supply these values and grabfood is a forbidden source for nutrient evidence. Two portions were present in the input (1 piece = 60 g; 3 pieces = 180 g); only the single-piece default (60 g) is used as the serving reference. The input had two entries both marked is_default: true, which is inconsistent; the 1-piece / 60 g portion is adopted as the canonical default. Ethnic tags 'Sashimi' and 'Japanese Rice Bowl' from the input are not applicable to ebi croquette and have been removed. Country of origin corrected from 'Singapore' to 'Japan' as ebi korokke is a Japanese yoshoku dish; Singapore is the serving market context only. Nutrient source: grabfood (forbidden) replaced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy composite (approved). GI source updated to cite Atkinson et al. 2021 AJCN / University of Sydney GI Database as the approved analogous-estimate basis.
